PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 2 — Interactive Map COMPLETE (1.1 + 1.2a + 1.2b + 1.2c shipped end-to-end)
LAST_DONE:    Sub-session 1.2c shipped and squash-merged. Squash commit on main: `c3f303f feat(map): Interactive Map feature — geo router + mapcn primitive + page route`. Final 1.2c work: (a) applied file-level `/* eslint-disable */` + `// @ts-nocheck` headers to the 1844-line mapcn vendor primitive at `apps/web/src/components/ui/map.tsx` (registry-managed, regenerated on `npx shadcn@latest add @mapcn/map` — inline fixes would be clobbered); (b) wired `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/map/page.tsx` server component to render `<InteractiveMap />` in a full-viewport rounded container; (c) governance — 2 new DECISIONS_LOG locked entries (mapcn vendor suppression, user dialog strict-mode lint deferral), CHANGELOG_AI + IMPLEMENTATION_MAP updated with full 4-sub-session record. Map is tile-only (CARTO basemap centered on Banda Sea [127.5, -2.5] zoom 6) — data-layer wiring of `map.getSubjects/getPatrolAreas/getEvents` into the InteractiveMap component is listed as follow-up. Visual QA deferred to user (server-side route verified serving `/map` with correct 307→/login?callbackUrl=/map auth gate). All 32 web router tests pass (including 10 map). pnpm typecheck clean.

              SIDE FIXES SURFACED DURING 1.2c VISUAL QA (stashed for next session, NOT merged to main):
              (a) `.env.dev` — added `AUTH_TRUST_HOST=true` (Auth.js v5 was rejecting localhost:45204 as untrusted host, silently breaking login redirects), and added `INTERNAL_DATABASE_URL` + `INTERNAL_REDIS_URL` using container hostnames `marine-guardian_dev_postgres:5432` / `marine-guardian_dev_valkey:6379` (the host-mapped URLs were unreachable from inside the app container — `localhost` inside the container resolves to itself, not the host).
              (b) `deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml` — added `DATABASE_URL: ${INTERNAL_DATABASE_URL}` + `REDIS_URL: ${INTERNAL_REDIS_URL}` environment overrides on BOTH `app` and `worker` services. (.env.dev is gitignored — local-only — but the compose change must land on a feature branch.) The compose change is in git stash@{0} "dev-docker-internal-urls-fix". The .env.dev edits are already in the working tree on the user's machine (not in git but present at runtime).

              WORKER STATE: `marine-guardian_dev_worker` is in a restart loop on a SEPARATE pre-existing issue — `ReferenceError: __dirname is not defined in ES module scope` in the bundled Prisma client (esbuild ESM bundling). NOT caused by 1.2c. App + login + map page do not depend on the worker. Mention in next-session triage but don't conflate with the docker URLs fix.

NEXT:         Three small follow-ups, recommended order:
